channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: RAG in Practice — Part 7: Your RAG System Is Wrong. Here's How to Find Out Why.
item.description: Part 7 of 8 — RAG Article Series Previous: RAG, Fine-Tuning, or Long Context? (Part 6) The Team That Blamed the Model TechNova's RAG system worked well at launch. Return policy questions got...

item.title: Why I Built a Visual Layer for Rails Migrations (And Why Reading Ruby DSL in a Diff Wasn't Enough)
item.description: Three months ago I merged a migration that added a null: false column with no default value. The table had 800k rows. Production broke, and the worst part wasn't the bug itself — it was that...

item.title: I took forg.to from DR 0 to DR 25 in 5 weeks. Here's the honest breakdown.
item.description: Six weeks ago forg.to had a domain rating of zero. Not "low." Not "needs improvement." Ahrefs showed a goose egg. Which meant no matter how good the content was, Google had zero reason to trust...

item.title: An index made our query faster. It slowly suffocated our database.
item.description: Hello, I'm Tuan. When backend engineers encounter a slow query, the first instinct is often something like: "Check the WHERE and ORDER BY, then just add a composite index." I used to think the...

item.title: Sleeping Barber Problem
item.description: Sleeping Barber Problem The sleeping barber problem is a classic concurrency problem introduced by Edsger Dijkstra in 1965. It illustrates the challenges of coordinating multiple processes sharing...

item.title: On Paralysis, Punishment, and Permission
item.description: the analysis loop — paralysis as a trap I’ve been squirming like a fly trapped under water — never still, never free. I obsessed over productivity tools. I consulted the smartest LLMs, hoping...

item.title: What Happens When You Type a URL in Your Browser?
item.description: You type a URL, hit Enter, and a webpage loads instantly. But behind that simple action lies a complex chain of events involving DNS, networking, routing, and security mechanisms. This article...

item.title: DeepSeek-V4: Towards Highly Efficient Million-Token Context Intelligence
item.description: Article URL: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47885014 Points: 43 # Comments: 3

item.title: We ran Qwen3.6-27B on $800 of consumer GPUs, day one: llama.cpp vs vLLM
item.description: Originally published at llmkube.com/blog/qwen3-6-27b-bakeoff. Cross-posted here for the dev.to audience. A Kubernetes-native bake-off on 2× RTX 5060 Ti, with reproducible manifests and a...

item.title: Refactoring My Life: How I Escaped a Broken Environment to Find the Terminal
item.description: Some say code is a sanctuary for programmers. For me, that isn't a metaphor—it was my survival protocol for the first 18 years of my life. 1. Namespace Conflict: The "Domestic Violence" Bug My...

item.title: US arrests soldier who allegedly made $400K on Maduro Polymarket bets
item.description: We knew someone made over $400,000 on suspicious Polymarket bets around the US operation to capture Venezuelan president Nicolas Maduro, but now we have a name: Gannon Ken Van Dyke. The US...

item.title: Leak reveals new Xbox Game Pass ‘Starter Edition’ that’s part of Discord Nitro
item.description: Microsoft started teasing a mysterious Discord and Xbox Game Pass partnership yesterday, and a new leak appears to have detailed exactly what's coming. Discord Previews has uncovered a new Xbox...

item.title: Claude is connecting directly to your personal apps like Spotify, Uber Eats, and TurboTax
item.description: Claude users can access more apps with Anthropic's AI now thanks to new connectors for everything from hiking to grocery shopping. Anthropic already supported connecting numerous work-related apps...

item.title: Brendan Carr’s war on wokeness targets inclusive children’s television
item.description: Under the guidance of consummate bully / chairman Brendan Carr, the FCC is taking steps towards cracking down on children's entertainment that in any way explores the complexities of gender...

item.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
item.description: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

item.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
item.description: Technically speaking, there's no practical benefit to use PQC. So why is it being used?

item.title: Sonos’ big sale on refurbished speakers is about to end
item.description: Portable audio and home theater upgrades can be very pricey, which is why we make a habit out of telling readers when there are good deals happening. With that in mind, here’s a friendly reminder...

item.title: Meta is laying off 10 percent of its staff
item.description: Meta is planning to layoff around 10 percent of employees in May, according to a memo from the company's chief people officer, Janelle Gale, published by Bloomberg. That means approximately 8,000...

item.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
item.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

item.title: Apple’s Next Chapter, SpaceX and Cursor Strike a Deal, and Palantir’s Controversial Manifesto
item.description: In this week’s episode of Uncanny Valley, we talk about Tim Cook’s legacy as CEO at Apple and what his long-rumored departure means for the future of one of the world's biggest companies.

item.title: ‘We Are Xbox’: read the memo defining Microsoft’s gaming future
item.description: Microsoft's new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has laid out a new strategy for what she describes as a "return of Xbox." In a joint memo with Xbox Chief Content Officer Matt Booty, Sharma and Booty lay out...

item.title: Microsoft’s new Xbox chief is ‘reevaluating’ exclusive games
item.description: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma held an all-hands meeting this morning and laid out her strategy for a "return of Xbox" with employees. While rebranding Microsoft Gaming back to Xbox was part of the agenda,...

item.title: Democrats want to ban ICE from turning warehouses into detention centers
item.description: A bill introduced by Rep. Rashida Tlaib (D-MI) would prohibit the Department of Homeland Security from converting warehouses and similar buildings into immigrant detention centers, an attempt to...

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
item.description: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.

item.title: At 'AI Coachella,' Stanford Students Line Up to Learn From Silicon Valley Royalty
item.description: CS 153 has gone viral on the Palo Alto campus—and on X. Not everyone is happy about it.

item.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Helpdesk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
item.description: A previously undocumented threat activity cluster known as UNC6692 has been observed leveraging social engineering tactics via Microsoft Teams to deploy a custom malware suite on compromised...

item.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
item.description: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants.

item.title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is in just a week. Now’s the time to grab yourself a ticket. Join VCs and founders at Sentro Filipino Cultural Center on April 30.

item.title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident
item.description: TechCrunch has confirmed that Delve was the compliance company that performed the security certifications for Context AI, the AI agent training startup that last week disclosed a security incident.

item.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
item.description: Bitwarden CLI has been compromised as part of the newly discovered and ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, according to new findings from JFrog and Socket. "The affected package version...
